title: Arenas, Pools, Slabs, and Fragmentation · C Contract Brief: Arena Bump Invariant
summary: A game simulation creates millions of short-lived entities and must trade individual release for bounded phase ownership. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem arena bump invariant increment in instrumented arena and object pool. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Implement checked alignment and bump arithmetic with capacity, high-water, reset, and out-of-memory evidence.
Apply ISO/IEC TS 6010 provenance concepts as reviewed guidance without mislabeling them as universally implemented C23 behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Arena Bump Invariant · Theory Arena Bump Invariant
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 8062. Produce bounded C evidence to maintain checked arena offset and high-water invariants; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Implement checked alignment and bump arithmetic with capacity, high-water, reset, and out-of-memory evidence.
